content:
# 大纲规划任务

项目仓库: NVlabs/cuda-oxide
项目名: cuda-oxide
讲义目录: cuda-oxide-tutorial/
模式: incremental
当前 HEAD: 29396b7f643b1d42eb4d80b7347ad27bb011525a
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/29396b7f643b1d42eb4d80b7347ad27bb011525a/
用户关注点: （无）

上次 HEAD: 2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d

## 现有大纲 manifest

```json
{
  "project": "cuda-oxide",
  "head": "2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d",
  "rationale": "本次增量更新覆盖 5 个 commit（#321–#326）：新增 cargo oxide sanitize 子命令（NVIDIA Compute Sanitizer）、打包原子加法 f16x2/bf16x2、bf16 m16n8k16 与 f64 m8n8k4 张量核 mma.sync intrinsic，并在设备 codegen 全链路贯通 --no-fmad（CUDA_OXIDE_NO_FMA），同时把 .oxart 制品升级到 v2 并新增 compile-options/FMA-contraction 策略。据此对已有讲义定级：U1 入门层仅 u1-l3（新增 sanitize 子命令）与 u1-l5（新增 packed_atomic_add 示例与 bf16/f64 mma intrinsic）需 update，u1-l1/u1-l2/u1-l4 keep；U2 设备编程 6 篇均未受影响 keep；U3 中 u3-l1 keep，u3-l2 因 .oxart 制品 v2 + compile-options 核心模型变化而 update。u3-l3、u3-l4 及 U4–U7 共 20 篇在上一版 manifest（于 u4-l1 处截断）中从未生成，本次补全为 new：U4 给出编译流水线地图（入口分流、MIR 导入、MLIR 方言层、lowering、NVVM→cubin/LTOIR 与 FMA 契约）；U5 聚焦 Hopper/Blackwell 高级设备能力并纳入本轮新增的打包原子与 bf16/f64 mma intrinsic；U6 深潜各编译阶段（含 #326 FMA 收缩）并以\\\"端到端新增 intrinsic\\\"模板收束（以 #321/#322/#323 为范本）；U7 收束于 compile_fail 安全契约、cuda-gdb、新增的 Compute Sanitizer 子命令与差分模糊测试。讲义间依赖：U5/U6 深潜均依赖 U4 地图，扩展类依赖对应实现层，调试类依赖 U1-L3 工具链；整体维持 7 单元 35 篇结构不变，逻辑优先于数量。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "入门：认识 cuda-oxide",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "项目定位与编译流水线总览",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "介绍 cuda-oxide 是什么：一个把纯 Rust 的 #[kernel] 函数编译成 CUDA PTX 的自定义 rustc 后端，实现单源（host/device 同文件）编译，并给出 Rust→MIR→Pliron IR→LLVM IR→PTX 的端到端流水线鸟瞰。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解项目定位、alpha 状态与适用场景",
            "画出从 Rust 源码到 GPU 上运行 PTX 的端到端流水线",
            "区分 host 路径（标准 LLVM 后端）与 device 路径（cuda-oxide 流水线）"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "crates/rustc-codegen-cuda/src/lib.rs"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "README 项目概述与状态",
            "rustc-codegen-cuda/lib.rs 的代码生成后端架构图",
            "host 与 device 分流",
            "端到端编译流水线"
          ],
          "practice_task": "阅读 README 与 rustc-codegen-cuda/src/lib.rs 顶部的 ASCII 架构图，用自己的话写出一段 Rust 代码从源码到 GPU PTX 经过的阶段，并标注哪些属于 host、哪些属于 device。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "keep"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l2",
          "title": "Workspace 与 crate 地图",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u1-l2-workspace-and-crate-map.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "解析 workspace 成员与三层 crate 划分：用户层（cuda-device/cuda-core/cuda-async/cuda-host/cuda-macros）、编译器层（dialect-mir/dialect-nvvm/mir-importer/mir-lower/mir-transforms/nvvm-transforms 等）、工具层（cargo-oxide），并说明为何 rustc-codegen-cuda 不在 workspace 内。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "说出每个 workspace 成员 crate 的职责",
            "区分用户层、编译器层、工具层三类 crate",
            "理解 rustc-codegen-cuda 为何需要特殊构建"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "Cargo.toml",
            "README.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "workspace members 列表",
            "用户面 crate 概览表",
            "编译器 crate 概览表",
            "rustc-codegen-cuda 的特殊构建说明"
          ],
          "practice_task": "对照 Cargo.toml 的 members 列表与 README 的 crate 概览表，绘制一张分层依赖草图，标注每个 crate 属于设备端/宿主端/编译器/工具哪一层。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "keep"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l3",
          "title": "工具链与 cargo-oxide 驱动",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u1-l3-toolchain-and-cargo-oxide.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解固定的 nightly 工具链与 CUDA/LLVM/clang 依赖，以及 cargo-oxide 子命令（run/build/pipeline/doctor/new/setup/debug/sanitize）如何驱动整个构建流程，并介绍 codegen 后端的发现顺序与 CUDA_OXIDE_* 环境变量；本轮新增 sanitize 子命令（SanitizerTool：memcheck/racecheck/initcheck/synccheck）。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "了解工具链与外部依赖要求",
            "掌握 cargo-oxide 各子命令（含 debug 与 sanitize）的用途",
            "理解 backend 发现顺序与环境变量配置"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "rust-toolchain.toml",
            ".cargo/config.toml",
            "crates/cargo-oxide/src/main.rs",
            "crates/cargo-oxide/README.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "rust-toolchain.toml 工具链",
            "cargo-oxide CLI 与子命令",
            "doctor/setup 环境校验",
            "backend 发现与 CUDA_OXIDE_* 变量",
            "sanitize 子命令与 SanitizerTool"
          ],
          "practice_task": "运行 `cargo oxide doctor`（或阅读其逻辑）列出本机缺失的前置依赖；再用 `cargo oxide new hello && cargo oxide run` 走通最小示例，并尝试 `cargo oxide sanitize hello --tool memcheck`。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "update"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l4",
          "title": "Hello GPU：vecadd 端到端",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u1-l4-hello-gpu-vecadd.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "以 vecadd 为最小编译单元，串起写 #[kernel]→cuda-oxide 编译→宿主加载→启动→回收结果的完整闭环，建立对单源编译模型的直观感受。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "读懂一个最小的 #[cuda_module] 内核与宿主 main",
            "跑通 vecadd 并核对 GPU 计算结果",
            "建立单源 host/device 编译的体感"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "crates/rustc-codegen-cuda/examples/vecadd/src/main.rs",
            "crates/rustc-codegen-cuda/src/lib.rs"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "#[cuda_module] mod 块",
            "#[kernel] fn vecadd",
            "宿主 main 与 DeviceBuffer",
            "to_host_vec 结果回收"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在 vecadd 基础上把核函数改成向量减法或数乘，重新 `cargo oxide run vecadd` 并核对输出正确。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l2",
            "u1-l3"
          ],
          "action": "keep"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l5",
          "title": "示例导览：cuda-oxide 能做什么",
          "order": 5,
          "filename": "u1-l5-examples-tour.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "用 examples/ 目录里 60+ 示例勾勒能力边界——泛型/闭包、原子、集群、异步、张量核（含本轮新增的 bf16 m16n8k16、f64 m8n8k4 mma.sync）、GEMM SoL、设备 FFI、打包原子加法（packed_atomic_add）等，帮读者选定后续学习方向。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "了解 examples 目录的组织与硬件要求",
            "建立项目能力矩阵的总体印象",
            "为后续学习选定切入点"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "crates/rustc-codegen-cuda/examples/async_vecadd/src/main.rs",
            "crates/rustc-codegen-cuda/examples/atomics/src/main.rs",
            "crates/rustc-codegen-cuda/examples/cluster/src/main.rs",
            "crates/rustc-codegen-cuda/examples/packed_atomic_add/src/main.rs"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "examples 目录与 README 示例表",
            "能力矩阵概览",
            "硬件要求（sm_xx）",
            "选型指引",
            "packed_atomic_add 等新增示例"
          ],
          "practice_task": "挑选 3 个示例（建议含 packed_atomic_add），分别用 `cargo oxide build`（无需 GPU）确认能编译，并为每个示例写一句话总结其演示的能力。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l4"
          ],
          "action": "update"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u2",
      "title": "进阶：编写 GPU 内核",
      "level": "intermediate",
      "order": 2,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u2-l1",
          "title": "#[kernel] 与 #[cuda_module] 宏",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u2-l1-kernel-and-cuda-module-macros.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "解析 #[cuda_module] 如何扫描模块里的 #[kernel] 函数、生成带类型的 launch 方法与 load() 加载器，以及保留符号命名约定如何把宏与 codegen 后端绑定。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 #[kernel] 与 #[cuda_module] 的展开结果",
            "说明 load() 如何加载并暴露带类型的启动方法",
            "认识宏与后端之间的保留符号契约"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "crates/cuda-macros/src/lib.rs",
            "crates/rustc-codegen-cuda/examples/vecadd/src/main.rs",
            "crates/reserved-oxide-symbols/src/lib.rs"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "#[kernel] 展开",
            "#[cuda_module] 生成 LoadedModule",
            "保留符号前缀",
            "load() 加载器"
          ],
          "practice_task": "给一个已有 #[kernel] 模块新增第二个核函数，观察宏自动生成了 module.<新核>(...) 方法，并解释它如何对应到 PTX 入口符号。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l4"
          ],
          "action": "keep"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l2",
          "title": "线程索引与类型安全",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u2-l2-thread-indexing-and-safety.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 thread::index_1d/2d 返回的 ThreadIndex 见证类型如何用生命周期与 !Copy/!Send/!Sync 在编译期防止线程索引被串用，以及 DisjointSlice::get_mut(idx) 的越界检查如何消除并行写入的数据竞争。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 ThreadIndex 见证类型与 Index1D/Index2D 索引空间",
            "说明类型系统如何在编译期阻止索引洗钱(laundering)与跨 scope 逃逸",
            "掌握 DisjointSlice 的越界安全访问模式"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "crates/cuda-device/src/thread.rs",
            "crates/cuda-device/src/disjoint.rs",
            "crates/cuda-device/src/lib.rs"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "ThreadIndex 见证类型",
            "Index1D/Index2D 索引空间",
            "DisjointSlice 类型安全访问",
            "越界与 laundering 防护"
          ],
          "practice_task": "故意写一段把 ThreadIndex 存进结构体跨 scope 使用的代码，观察编译失败信息；再用 DisjointSlice::get_mut 重写一个越界安全的写入。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "keep"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l3",
          "title": "共享内存与同步",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u2-l3-shared-memory-and-sync.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "介绍 SharedArray<T,N> 静态共享内存与 DynamicSharedArray 动态共享内存的声明与使用，以及 sync_threads、threadfence 系列内存栅栏如何配合共享内存做线程块内协作。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "声明并使用静态/动态共享内存",
            "理解 sync_threads 块级屏障的语义",
            "掌握 threadfence 系列栅栏的作用域差异"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "crates/cuda-device/src/shared.rs",
            "crates/cuda-device/src/fence.rs",
            "crates/rustc-codegen-cuda/examples/addressof_sharedarray/src/main.rs"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "SharedArray 声明",
            "DynamicSharedArray",
            "sync_threads",
            "threadfence 系列"
          ],
          "practice_task": "写一个核函数：每个线程把自己的线程号写入共享内存，sync_threads 后让线程 0 求和并写回全局内存。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l2"
          ],
          "action": "keep"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l4",
          "title": "从宿主启动内核",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u2-l4-launching-kernels.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 LaunchConfig（grid/block/shared）、LaunchConfig::for_num_elems 的便捷构造，以及 cuda-core/cuda-host 如何把参数压栈并调用 cuLaunchKernel。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 LaunchConfig 的字段与 grid/block 含义",
            "掌握 for_num_elems 的自动配置推导",
            "了解参数 marshalling 与 cuLaunchKernel 调用"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "crates/cuda-core/src/launch.rs",
            "crates/cuda-host/src/launch.rs",
            "crates/cuda-core/src/lib.rs"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "LaunchConfig 结构",
            "for_num_elems 推导",
            "参数 marshalling",
            "cuLaunchKernel 调用"
          ],
          "practice_task": "手动构造一个 LaunchConfig（grid/block）启动 vecadd，再对比 for_num_elems 自动算出的配置，理解 grid/block 映射关系。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "keep"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l5",
          "title": "设备内存与数据搬运",
          "order": 5,
          "filename": "u2-l5-device-memory-and-data-movement.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "介绍 DeviceBuffer<T> 的 RAII 管理、from_host/to_host_vec/zeroed 等搬运 API、DeviceCopy trait，以及 PinnedHostBuffer 锁页内存对传输带宽的意义。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "使用 DeviceBuffer 进行设备内存分配与搬运",
            "理解 DeviceCopy trait 的约束",
            "认识锁页内存对传输性能的作用"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "crates/cuda-core/src/device_buffer.rs",
            "crates/cuda-core/src/memory.rs",
            "crates/cuda-core/src/pinned_host_buffer.rs"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "DeviceBuffer RAII",
            "from_host/to_host_vec/zeroed",
            "DeviceCopy trait",
            "PinnedHostBuffer"
          ],
          "practice_task": "用 PinnedHostBuffer 作为中转，写一个 host→device→host 的搬运核函数，对比普通 DeviceBuffer 路径体会带宽差异。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l4"
          ],
          "action": "keep"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l6",
          "title": "闭包与泛型上 GPU",
          "order": 6,
          "filename": "u2-l6-closures-and-generics.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解泛型核函数的单态化（每个具体类型生成独立 PTX 入口）、闭包捕获如何被标量化成 kernel 参数，以及 type_id_u128 如何为泛型核生成稳定的 PTX 名。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解泛型核的单态化与多入口生成",
            "说明闭包捕获如何被标量化传入 kernel",
            "了解 type_id_u128 在泛型 kernel 命名中的作用"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "crates/rustc-codegen-cuda/examples/host_closure/src/main.rs",
            "crates/rustc-codegen-cuda/examples/generic/src/main.rs",
            "crates/cuda-host/src/type_id.rs",
            "README.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "泛型单态化",
            "闭包捕获标量化",
            "type_id_u128",
            "host_closure/generic 示例"
          ],
          "practice_task": "基于 host_closure 示例，写一个泛型 map<T,F> 核函数，分别用 f32 和 i32 启动，确认生成了两个不同 PTX 入口。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "keep"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u3",
      "title": "进阶：宿主运行时",
      "level": "intermediate",
      "order": 3,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u3-l1",
          "title": "cuda-core 安全封装",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u3-l1-cuda-core-safe-wrappers.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "解析 CudaContext/CudaStream/CudaModule/CudaEvent 等 RAII 类型如何安全包裹 CUDA Driver API，包括上下文绑定、流的 fork/join 与事件同步。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 CudaContext/CudaStream 的 RAII 生命周期",
            "使用 CudaEvent 做流间同步与计时",
            "理解安全封装背后的 Driver API 映射"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "crates/cuda-core/src/context.rs",
            "crates/cuda-core/src/stream.rs",
            "crates/cuda-core/src/module.rs",
            "crates/cuda-core/src/event.rs"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "CudaContext",
            "CudaStream fork/join",
            "CudaModule",
            "CudaEvent"
          ],
          "practice_task": "创建两个 stream，分别启动核函数，用 CudaEvent 测量并对比它们的执行时间。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l4"
          ],
          "action": "keep"
        },
        {
          "id": "u3-l2",
          "title": "模块加载与内嵌制品",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u3-l2-module-loading-and-embedded-artifacts.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解编译期把 PTX 打包成 .oxart 制品段、链接锚符号防止被 dead-strip、运行时 cuda-host 从当前可执行文件中发现并加载内嵌 bundle 的全过程；本轮 .oxart 升级到 v2，新增 ArtifactCompileOptions（FMA 收缩策略）与 .options/.target 边车文件，并保持对 v1 的向后兼容。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 .oxart 制品 wire 格式与 entry 记录",
            "说明锚符号如何防止 lib crate 的 bundle 被裁剪",
            "追踪运行时 bundle 发现与加载流程",
            "理解 v2 制品的 compile-options/FMA-contraction 策略"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "crates/cuda-host/src/embedded.rs",
            "crates/cuda-core/src/embedded.rs",
            "crates/oxide-artifacts/src/lib.rs",
            "crates/rustc-codegen-cuda/src/lib.rs"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            ".oxart bundle 格式（v1/v2）",
            "artifact 锚符号",
            "运行时 bundle 发现",
            "load_embedded_module",
            "ArtifactCompileOptions 与 FMA 边车"
          ],

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
===== cuda-macros README (launch contract + nested modules) =====
# cuda-macros

Procedural macros for writing CUDA kernels in Rust. Provides `#[cuda_module]`
for typed embedded-module loading, `#[kernel]` for GPU entry points,
`#[device]`, `#[launch_bounds]`, `#[cluster_launch]`, `#[cooperative_launch]`,
`#[launch_contract]`, `gpu_printf!`, `ptx_asm!`, and the lower-level `cuda_launch!` / `cuda_launch_async!`
escape hatches. Both lower-level launch macros are caller-unsafe: prefer
`#[cuda_module]` with a launch contract unless you are launching a module
loaded at runtime by name.

## Attributes

### `#[kernel]` -- GPU Kernel Entry Point

Marks a function as a CUDA kernel. Generates:
1. An entry point renamed into the reserved `cuda_oxide_kernel_<hash>_<name>` namespace
   (with `#[no_mangle]`) so the codegen backend can find it. The hash makes the prefix
   unguessable for user code; see `crates/reserved-oxide-symbols/` for the contract.
2. Host lookup metadata used by typed launch APIs.
3. For a generic kernel, a readable `<name>_ptx_name::<...>()` helper. Generated
   marker types are internal plumbing and should not be named by application code.

The `<name>_ptx_name` sibling is part of the generated API, so that name must
remain free beside a generic kernel. Calling it also retains that concrete
specialization in device output; it cannot return a name for an omitted entry.

> **Reserved names.** The macros refuse to compile any function whose name starts with
> `cuda_oxide_` -- that namespace is reserved for cuda-oxide-internal mangling. The check
> is enforced at expansion time so the error points at the offending source line.

```rust
use cuda_device::{kernel, DisjointSlice};

#[kernel]
pub fn vecadd(a: &[f32], b: &[f32], mut c: DisjointSlice<f32>) {
    if let Some((c_elem, idx)) = c.get_mut_indexed() {
        let i = idx.get();
        *c_elem = a[i] + b[i];
    }
}
```

**Generic kernels** work in two modes:

```rust
// Mode 1: call-site specialization (PTX name from the function-item TypeId)
#[kernel]
pub fn scale<T: Copy + Mul<Output = T>>(factor: T, input: &[T], mut out: DisjointSlice<T>) { ... }
// Raw launch: unsafe { module.scale::<f32>(&stream, config, factor, &input, &mut out)? }
// Inspect its generated entry name: scale_ptx_name::<f32>()

// Mode 2: explicit instantiation list
#[kernel(f32, i32)]
pub fn scale<T: Copy + Mul<Output = T>>(factor: T, input: &[T], mut out: DisjointSlice<T>) { ... }
// Generates named entry points: scale_f32, scale_i32
```

The legacy explicit list supports exactly one type parameter. Use Mode 1 for
const parameters, lifetimes, or mixed type/const kernels.

### `#[device]` -- Device Helper Functions and Externs

Device functions run on GPU but are not entry points. Works on both regular functions and `extern "C"` blocks:

```rust
#[device]
pub fn magnitude(x: f32, y: f32) -> f32 {
    (x * x + y * y).sqrt()
}

// Extern device functions (e.g. from libdevice or cuBLASDx)
#[device]
extern "C" {
    fn __nv_expf(x: f32) -> f32;
}
```

| Feature              | `#[kernel]`          | `#[device]`          |
|----------------------|----------------------|----------------------|
| Entry point          | Yes (PTX `.entry`)   | No (PTX `.func`)     |
| Can return values    | No (must be `()`)    | Yes                  |
| Callable from host   | Via `#[cuda_module]` | No                   |
| Callable from device | Yes                  | Yes                  |

### `#[launch_bounds(max_threads, min_blocks)]`

Occupancy hints for register allocation. It may appear before or after
`#[kernel]`; generic kernels forward its compiler marker to every generated
entry.

```rust
#[kernel]
#[launch_bounds(256, 2)]  // max 256 threads, min 2 blocks per SM
pub fn optimized(out: DisjointSlice<f32>) { ... }
// PTX: .entry optimized .maxntid 256 .minnctapersm 2 { ... }
```

### `#[launch_contract(...)]`

Declares a kernel's launch-time correctness assumptions. Inside
`#[cuda_module]`, this changes that kernel's generated API from a raw
`LaunchConfig` to a prepared, specialization-branded launch:

```rust
#[kernel]
#[launch_bounds(256)]
#[launch_contract(
    domain = 1,
    block = (256, 1, 1),
    dynamic_shared_range = (1024, 49152),
    dynamic_shared_alignment = 128,
    min_compute_capability = (9, 0),
)]
pub fn reduce(input: &[f32], mut out: DisjointSlice<f32>) { /* ... */ }

let prepared = module.prepare_reduce(LaunchConfig1D::new(blocks, 256, 8192))?;
module.reduce(&stream, &prepared, &input, &mut out)?;
```

Kernel configuration attributes may appear before or after `#[kernel]`. If an
attribute expands first, generic kernel generation forwards its exact internal
marker to the exported entry.

```text
prepare_reduce: dimensions + live CUDA limits -> PreparedLaunch<reduce>
reduce:         PreparedLaunch<reduce>         -> enqueue
reduce_unchecked: raw LaunchConfig             -> unsafe expert path
```

`block` is exact. If it is omitted, `#[launch_bounds]` supplies the compiled
maximum total threads per block. For example, a limit of 256 accepts both
`(256, 1, 1)` and `(16, 16, 1)`. Dynamic shared memory is either exact
(`dynamic_shared = BYTES`) or an inclusive range. The byte extent is an author
promise; the alignment is a compiler-visible minimum and is merged with any
higher `DynamicSharedArray<T, ALIGN>` request in the body or a reachable local
helper. Prelinked external helpers retain their separately compiled alignment.
Contract values are integer literals in this initial API; specialization const
expressions are not accepted yet.

`domain` is deliberately explicit because calls through device helpers defeat
AST inference. `#[cuda_module]` adds a sealed trait bound to the complete
`DisjointSlice` parameter type, so Rust resolves aliases before checking the
rank:

```text
type Tile = Index2D<64>;  DisjointSlice<_, Tile>    + domain 2 -> accepted
type Tile = Index1D;      DisjointSlice<_, Tile>    + domain 2 -> type error
local struct named DisjointSlice                     -> type error
```

Mutable slice parameters, incompatible cluster axes, and blocks above the
emitted launch bounds are also rejected.

Opted-in kernels gain generated sync, borrowed-async, and owned-async safe
methods that consume the same prepared proof. Their raw paths use an
`_unchecked` suffix. Uncontracted methods keep their existing names, but are
also unsafe because no proof ties their raw configuration to the kernel.

All loaders for a module containing a contracted kernel are unsafe. Bundles are
currently identified at package granularity, so `load()` cannot distinguish a
library artifact from a same-package binary artifact by name alone. The caller
must prove that `load`, `load_async`, `load_named`, `load_async_named`, or
`from_module` binds code with the ABI and resource semantics declared by the
module. Generic loading also merges all PTX bundles, so those specializations
must match and have no conflicting entry definitions. Preparation and launch
are safe after this one-time binding.

Cluster and cooperative requirements are each checked against the live device.
Combining them currently fails preparation because cuda-oxide cannot yet prove
the combined residency limit with the available occupancy query.

### `#[cluster_launch(x, y, z)]`

Compile-time thread block cluster dimensions (Hopper+). It may appear before or
after `#[kernel]`.

```rust
#[kernel]
#[cluster_launch(4, 1, 1)]  // 4 blocks per cluster
pub fn cluster_kernel(out: DisjointSlice<u32>) { ... }
// PTX: .entry cluster_kernel .reqnctapercluster 4, 1, 1 { ... }
```

### `#[cooperative_launch]`

Marks a kernel for cooperative launch, the precondition for grid-wide
barriers (`cuda_device::grid::sync()`). It may appear before or after
`#[kernel]`. Unlike `#[cluster_launch]` this changes nothing in the PTX:
`#[cuda_module]` records the setting before nested attributes expand and
routes every generated launch method through `cuLaunchKernelEx` with
`CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_COOPERATIVE` set. May be
combined with `#[cluster_launch]`; both attributes then go into the same
`cuLaunchKernelEx` call.

```rust
#[kernel]
#[cooperative_launch]
pub fn grid_sync_kernel(mut out: DisjointSlice<u32>) {
    // ... per-block work ...
    grid::sync();

pub fn reduce(input: &[f32], mut out: DisjointSlice<f32>) { /* ... */ }

let prepared = module.prepare_reduce(LaunchConfig1D::new(blocks, 256, 8192))?;
module.reduce(&stream, &prepared, &input, &mut out)?;
```

Kernel configuration attributes may appear before or after `#[kernel]`. If an
attribute expands first, generic kernel generation forwards its exact internal
marker to the exported entry.

```text
prepare_reduce: dimensions + live CUDA limits -> PreparedLaunch<reduce>
reduce:         PreparedLaunch<reduce>         -> enqueue
reduce_unchecked: raw LaunchConfig             -> unsafe expert path
```

`block` is exact. If it is omitted, `#[launch_bounds]` supplies the compiled
maximum total threads per block. For example, a limit of 256 accepts both
`(256, 1, 1)` and `(16, 16, 1)`. Dynamic shared memory is either exact
(`dynamic_shared = BYTES`) or an inclusive range. The byte extent is an author
promise; the alignment is a compiler-visible minimum and is merged with any
higher `DynamicSharedArray<T, ALIGN>` request in the body or a reachable local
helper. Prelinked external helpers retain their separately compiled alignment.
Contract values are integer literals in this initial API; specialization const
expressions are not accepted yet.

`domain` is deliberately explicit because calls through device helpers defeat
AST inference. `#[cuda_module]` adds a sealed trait bound to the complete
`DisjointSlice` parameter type, so Rust resolves aliases before checking the
rank:

```text
type Tile = Index2D<64>;  DisjointSlice<_, Tile>    + domain 2 -> accepted
type Tile = Index1D;      DisjointSlice<_, Tile>    + domain 2 -> type error
local struct named DisjointSlice                     -> type error
```

Mutable slice parameters, incompatible cluster axes, and blocks above the
emitted launch bounds are also rejected.

Opted-in kernels gain generated sync, borrowed-async, and owned-async safe
methods that consume the same prepared proof. Their raw paths use an
`_unchecked` suffix. Uncontracted methods keep their existing names, but are
also unsafe because no proof ties their raw configuration to the kernel.

All loaders for a module containing a contracted kernel are unsafe. Bundles are
currently identified at package granularity, so `load()` cannot distinguish a
library artifact from a same-package binary artifact by name alone. The caller
must prove that `load`, `load_async`, `load_named`, `load_async_named`, or
`from_module` binds code with the ABI and resource semantics declared by the
module. Generic loading also merges all PTX bundles, so those specializations
must match and have no conflicting entry definitions. Preparation and launch
are safe after this one-time binding.

Cluster and cooperative requirements are each checked against the live device.
Combining them currently fails preparation because cuda-oxide cannot yet prove
the combined residency limit with the available occupancy query.

### `#[cluster_launch(x, y, z)]`

Compile-time thread block cluster dimensions (Hopper+). It may appear before or
after `#[kernel]`.

```rust
#[kernel]
#[cluster_launch(4, 1, 1)]  // 4 blocks per cluster
pub fn cluster_kernel(out: DisjointSlice<u32>) { ... }
// PTX: .entry cluster_kernel .reqnctapercluster 4, 1, 1 { ... }
```

### `#[cooperative_launch]`

Marks a kernel for cooperative launch, the precondition for grid-wide
barriers (`cuda_device::grid::sync()`). It may appear before or after
`#[kernel]`. Unlike `#[cluster_launch]` this changes nothing in the PTX:
`#[cuda_module]` records the setting before nested attributes expand and
routes every generated launch method through `cuLaunchKernelEx` with
`CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_COOPERATIVE` set. May be
combined with `#[cluster_launch]`; both attributes then go into the same
`cuLaunchKernelEx` call.

```rust
#[kernel]
#[cooperative_launch]
pub fn grid_sync_kernel(mut out: DisjointSlice<u32>) {
    // ... per-block work ...
    grid::sync();

===== nested modules in cuda-macros README =====
189:`#[cuda_module]` records the setting before nested attributes expand and

===== cuda_module_nested example README =====
# cuda_module_nested

Demonstrates `#[cuda_module]` collecting kernels from nested inline modules.
Each Rust namespace gets a launcher view for the kernels defined there:

```text
kernels::LoadedModule
├── kernels::init::LoadedModule
├── kernels::scale::LoadedModule
├── kernels::offset::LoadedModule
└── kernels::post::LoadedModule
    └── kernels::post::double::LoadedModule
```

Build a child view from its immediate parent. The views share the same loaded
CUDA module and generic-function cache:

```rust,ignore
let root = kernels::load(&ctx)?;
let scale = kernels::scale::LoadedModule::from_parent(&root)?;
scale.scale_by(&stream, config, &input, &mut output)?;
```

Only inline module bodies are visible to an attribute macro. `mod child;` and
`include!` items are left untouched, but kernels behind those boundaries do
not receive generated launchers. Kernel function names must also remain unique
across the tree because PTX entry symbols are not namespace-qualified yet.

## Run

```
cargo oxide run cuda_module_nested
```

Expected output:

```
✓ SUCCESS: root-loaded nested inline kernels all ran
```

===== new wmma intrinsics in cuda-device/src/wmma.rs =====
@@ -214,6 +214,131 @@ pub unsafe fn mma_m16n8k16_f32_bf16(c: [f32; 4], a: [u32; 4], b: [u32; 2]) -> [f
     unreachable!("mma_m16n8k16_f32_bf16 called outside CUDA kernel context")
+/// Multiply one warp-distributed F16 tile and add an f32 accumulator.
+/// Together, the 32 lanes compute `D = A × B + C` for row-major `A` with
+/// shape 16×16, column-major `B` with shape 16×8, and `C`/`D` with shape
+/// 16×8. Each lane supplies its fragments in registers and receives four f32
+/// result registers. The call itself does not access memory or act as a fence.
+/// The Rust arrays use the same order as the PTX register lists below:
+/// - `c[0..4]` contains `%c0..%c3`, one `.f32` accumulator per element. The
+///   returned array contains `%d0..%d3` in the same order.
+/// - `a[0..4]` contains `%a0..%a3`, and `b[0..2]` contains `%b0..%b1`.
+///   Each `u32` is a raw `.b32` carrier holding two `.f16` values; element
+///   `j` is in `a[j / 2]` or `b[j / 2]`, low 16 bits before high 16 bits.
+/// For lane `lane`, let `group = lane / 4` and `thread = lane % 4`:
+/// ```text
+/// A element j=0..7:
+///   row = group       for j in {0,1,4,5}; otherwise group + 8
+///   col = thread*2 + (j&1) + (if j >= 4 { 8 } else { 0 })
+/// B element j=0..3:
+///   row = thread*2 + (j&1) + (if j >= 2 { 8 } else { 0 })
+///   col = group
+/// C/D register j=0..3:
+///   row = group + (if j >= 2 { 8 } else { 0 })
+///   col = thread*2 + (j&1)
+/// ```
+/// These coordinates are the `.row.col` layout: A is row-major and B is
+/// column-major.
+/// # PTX
+/// ```ptx
+/// mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k16.row.col.f32.f16.f16.f32
+///     {%d0, %d1, %d2, %d3},
+///     {%a0, %a1, %a2, %a3},
+///     {%b0, %b1},
+///     {%c0, %c1, %c2, %c3};
+/// ```
+/// # Safety
+/// - All 32 lanes must execute the same `mma.sync.aligned` instruction with
+///   the same qualifiers. Calling from divergent control flow, or after any
+///   lane has exited, is undefined behavior.
+/// - `c`, `a`, and `b` must contain the calling lane's fragments in the layout

commit 932183a6ec212d1d8afe460cfbebf88ec8218603
Author: Mohamed Samir <94049545+mohamedsamirx@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Sat Jul 4 10:29:07 2026 +0300

    Fix cargo oxide debug executable discovery (#330)
    
    * Fix cargo oxide debug executable discovery
    
    Reuse Cargo compiler-artifact JSON parsing for debug builds so the debugger launches the executable Cargo actually produced instead of guessing target/release/<example>.

    * Address cargo debug artifact review feedback
    
    Signed-off-by: Mohamed Samir <mohamedsamirx@users.noreply.github.com>
    
    * fix(cargo-oxide): harden Cargo artifact selection

diff --git a/crates/mir-importer/README.md b/crates/mir-importer/README.md
index 40a728f..b28e158 100644
--- a/crates/mir-importer/README.md
+++ b/crates/mir-importer/README.md
@@ -1,29 +1,24 @@
 # mir-importer
 
-Rust MIR to `dialect-mir` translator and compilation pipeline for cuda-oxide.
+Rust MIR to `dialect-mir` translator for cuda-oxide.
 
 Translates rustc's Stable MIR into [`dialect-mir`](../dialect-mir/) (a pliron
-dialect, MLIR-like) using the alloca + load/store model, then orchestrates the
-rest of the pipeline through `mem2reg`, annotated loop unrolling, lowering to
-the LLVM dialect (provided by `pliron-llvm`), LLVM IR export, and PTX generation
-via `llc`.
+dialect, MLIR-like) using the alloca + load/store model, then calls the shared
+`cuda-oxide-codegen` backend for preparation, lowering, export, and PTX or NVVM
+IR generation.
 
 ## Architecture
 
 ```text
-┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
-│                           mir-importer                                  │
-├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
-│                                                                         │
-│  ┌─────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────┐  │

Now I have a comprehensive understanding of all the changes. Let me now summarize the impact on each lecture and decide the actions.

## Summary of changes (8 commits, 2b713541 → 29396b7):

### Major features:
1. **#318 Typed kernel launch contracts** (HUGE):
   - `#[launch_contract(...)]` attribute: declares domain/block/shared/compute-cap assumptions
   - Generates `module.prepare_<kernel>()` → `PreparedLaunch<kernel>` (specialization-branded)
   - `LaunchConfig` becomes raw/unsafe; new typed `LaunchConfig1D/2D/3D` (sealed `KernelLaunchConfig`)
   - All loaders for a contracted module are unsafe; uncontracted launch methods also unsafe
   - Branding in cuda-device (disjoint.rs, thread.rs, shared.rs) — sealed trait bounds
   - New compile_fail tests: fail_private_construction, fail_private_mutation, fail_wrong_brand, fail_wrong_rank, fake_disjoint_slice, misleading_index_alias, etc.
   - cuda-host launch.rs, cuda-async launch.rs, cuda-macros lib.rs heavily changed
   - `reduce_unchecked` raw path; safe sync/borrowed-async/owned-async methods
   - **Nearly ALL examples changed** to adopt unsafe launch wrappers

2. **#314 cuda-oxide-codegen** (new crate, major architecture):
   - New `cuda-oxide-codegen` crate: rustc-independent dialect-mir → PTX backend
   - Public surface: `experimental::{CodegenModule, CompileOptions, Compiler, Target}`
   - Extracts post-translation pipeline tail from mir-importer
   - mir-importer now calls shared cuda-oxide-codegen backend
   - Files: api.rs, export.rs, lower.rs, options.rs, pipeline.rs, ptx.rs, target.rs, verify.rs, prep.rs, error.rs, llvm_tools.rs
   - New tests: compile_to_ptx.rs, concurrent_compile.rs, spine_kernel_ptx.rs

3. **#324 Nested modules** (#cuda_module collects kernels from nested inline modules):
   - `#[cuda_module]` now collects kernels from nested inline modules
   - Child `LoadedModule` views via `from_parent`
   - New example `cuda_module_nested`
   - Kernel names must be unique across tree
   - New compile_fail tests for boundaries

4. **#327/#328/#329 new mma.sync intrinsics**:
   - f16 m16n8k16 (`mma_m16n8k16_f32_f16`)
   - tf32 m16n8k8 (`mma_m16n8k8_f32_tf32`)
   - s8 m16n8k32 (`mma_m16n8k32_s32_s8`)
   - Changes in: cuda-device/wmma.rs, dialect-nvvm/ops/wmma.rs, mir-importer/translator/terminator/intrinsics/wmma.rs, mir-lower/convert/intrinsics/wmma.rs

5. **#330 cargo-oxide debug fix**: debug now uses Cargo compiler-artifact JSON; added --bin/--features; resolves cuda-gdb via CUDA toolkit roots.

6. **#331 licensing**: unified Apache-2.0; LICENSE-NVIDIA removed; cuda-bindings now Apache too.
